key_insight: A high-throughput MLIP- and CALPHAD-driven workflow can rapidly map spinodal decomposition risk and predict microstructure evolution in high-dimensional alloy composition spaces using analytic Gibbs-energy derivatives.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster alloy design by screening composition regions for coherent spinodal stability and linking thermodynamics directly to phase-field morphology predictions.
method: Train MLIPs to obtain thermodynamic models and elastic constants at high throughput.
Use CALPHAD-based Gibbs energy Hessians analytically to evaluate stability more efficiently and accurately than finite differences.
Feed the resulting thermodynamic and elastic inputs into elasto-chemical phase-field simulations for morphology prediction.
evidence_signal: The workflow is demonstrated on the Hf-Nb-Ti-V quaternary system, showing stability analysis and microstructure prediction from the abstract.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the quality and transferability of the MLIP and CALPHAD inputs, especially when extrapolating across complex composition spaces.
paper_title: From MLIPs to Microstructure: A High-Throughput Computational Framework to Design Spinodal Alloys in High-Dimensional Composition Spaces via Analytic Derivatives of CALPHAD Model Predictions
